ectoStrength.name: Compound strength (ectomorph)
ectoStrength.summary: Push / Pull / Legs split — detailed work by muscle group for mass gain.
ectoStrength.description: Classic PPL split hits each muscle group 2×/week with compound lifts first, isolation second. Rest 48–72 h before training the same group again (ACSM). Aim for progressive overload: +2.5 kg or +1 rep when all sets completed cleanly.
warmup: 5 min rowing or bike — raise heart rate
Arm circles, band pull-aparts, bodyweight squats × 10
Ramp-up sets: empty bar → 50% working weight → working sets
weeklySplit: Wed — Legs (quads, hamstrings, glutes, calves, core)

chest.note: Push day A — horizontal pressing priority
chest.detail: Mass-gain split: log loads and add weight or reps weekly on compounds. Order: compound press first, then incline, finish with fly/isolation. Rest as noted between heavy sets.

shoulders.detail: Mass-gain split: log loads and add weight or reps weekly on compounds. Press for strength, raises for volume, rear-delt work for shoulder health. Keep wrists neutral.

triceps.note: Finish push day with triceps isolation
triceps.detail: Mass-gain split: log loads and add weight or reps weekly on compounds. Mix pressing and extension patterns. Lock out under control without flaring elbows.

back.detail: Mass-gain split: log loads and add weight or reps weekly on compounds. Pair vertical and horizontal pulls. Squeeze shoulder blades; avoid swinging or using momentum.

biceps.note: Pull day — avoid swinging, full ROM
biceps.detail: Mass-gain split: log loads and add weight or reps weekly on compounds. Full range of motion, elbows fixed. Two to three exercises per session is enough.

quads.detail: Mass-gain split: log loads and add weight or reps weekly on compounds. Track knees over toes. Depth depends on mobility — keep a neutral spine.

hamstrings.note: Leg day — hip hinge after squats
hamstrings.detail: Mass-gain split: log loads and add weight or reps weekly on compounds. Hinge at the hips on RDL/deadlift patterns. Feel stretch, then drive hips forward.

glutes.detail: Mass-gain split: log loads and add weight or reps weekly on compounds. Pause and squeeze at lockout. Avoid hyperextending the lower back.

calves.note: Full stretch at bottom each rep
calves.detail: Mass-gain split: log loads and add weight or reps weekly on compounds. Full stretch at the bottom, peak contraction at the top. Use a slow tempo.

core.detail: Mass-gain split: log loads and add weight or reps weekly on compounds. Brace before each rep; breathe steadily. Prioritize form over speed or extra reps.

steps: Track loads in app or notebook — aim +1 rep/week on main lifts
Protein 1.6–1.8 g/kg within 2 h post-workout
Deload every 6–8 weeks: −40% volume for recovery
tips: Never skip leg day — lower body drives total mass gain
Sleep 7–9 h; ectomorphs recover fast but need calories
Stop 1–2 reps before failure on heavy compounds

ectoCardio.name: Moderate cardio (ectomorph)
ectoCardio.summary: Lower-body cardio focus — heart health without muscle loss.
ectoCardio.description: Cardio primarily loads legs and core stabilizers. Keep sessions sub-maximal to preserve caloric surplus. WHO: 150 min/week minimum; ectomorphs stay at lower end during bulk phases.
warmup: Leg swings front/back and side — 10 each
weeklySplit: Tue — 35 min brisk walk or cycling (Zone 2)
Sat — optional 15 min HIIT (not on leg day)

quads.note: Primary drivers in cycling and incline walking
items: Stationary bike — 25–35 min steady, moderate resistance
quads.detail: Keep intensity submaximal to protect muscle; stay in Zone 2 for most minutes. Track knees over toes. Depth depends on mobility — keep a neutral spine.

items: Elliptical with active heel push — 20 min
hamstrings.detail: Keep intensity submaximal to protect muscle; stay in Zone 2 for most minutes. Hinge at the hips on RDL/deadlift patterns. Feel stretch, then drive hips forward.

glutes.detail: Keep intensity submaximal to protect muscle; stay in Zone 2 for most minutes. Pause and squeeze at lockout. Avoid hyperextending the lower back.

calves.note: Running and jumping — keep volume low
items: Calf raise holds on step — 2×30 s
calves.detail: Keep intensity submaximal to protect muscle; stay in Zone 2 for most minutes. Full stretch at the bottom, peak contraction at the top. Use a slow tempo.

core.detail: Keep intensity submaximal to protect muscle; stay in Zone 2 for most minutes. Brace before each rep; breathe steadily. Prioritize form over speed or extra reps.

steps: Keep HR in Zone 2 — talk test: full sentences OK
Separate HIIT from leg day by 24 h
Add 200–300 kcal on cardio days if bulking
tips: Fasted cardio only if energy is fine — eat if dizzy
Prefer cycling over long runs to spare joints
Hydrate 500 ml per 30 min sweat

mesoMixed.name: Upper / Lower + HIIT (mesomorph)
mesoMixed.description: Upper/lower allows high volume per muscle group while hitting everything 2×/week. HIIT day targets legs and core with power movements. Ideal mesomorph frequency per ACSM combined training guidelines.
weeklySplit: Mon — Upper A (chest emphasis, back, arms)
Tue — Lower A (quad emphasis, glutes, core)
Thu — Upper B (back emphasis, shoulders, arms)
Fri — Lower B (hinge emphasis, hamstrings, calves)

chest.detail: Upper/lower rotation: push carbs on lower days, prioritize sleep for recovery. Order: compound press first, then incline, finish with fly/isolation. Rest as noted between heavy sets.

back.detail: Upper/lower rotation: push carbs on lower days, prioritize sleep for recovery. Pair vertical and horizontal pulls. Squeeze shoulder blades; avoid swinging or using momentum.

shoulders.detail: Upper/lower rotation: push carbs on lower days, prioritize sleep for recovery. Press for strength, raises for volume, rear-delt work for shoulder health. Keep wrists neutral.

triceps.note: Superset with biceps on upper days
triceps.detail: Upper/lower rotation: push carbs on lower days, prioritize sleep for recovery. Mix pressing and extension patterns. Lock out under control without flaring elbows.

biceps.detail: Upper/lower rotation: push carbs on lower days, prioritize sleep for recovery. Full range of motion, elbows fixed. Two to three exercises per session is enough.

quads.detail: Upper/lower rotation: push carbs on lower days, prioritize sleep for recovery. Track knees over toes. Depth depends on mobility — keep a neutral spine.

hamstrings.detail: Upper/lower rotation: push carbs on lower days, prioritize sleep for recovery. Hinge at the hips on RDL/deadlift patterns. Feel stretch, then drive hips forward.

glutes.detail: Upper/lower rotation: push carbs on lower days, prioritize sleep for recovery. Pause and squeeze at lockout. Avoid hyperextending the lower back.

calves.detail: Upper/lower rotation: push carbs on lower days, prioritize sleep for recovery. Full stretch at the bottom, peak contraction at the top. Use a slow tempo.

core.detail: Upper/lower rotation: push carbs on lower days, prioritize sleep for recovery. Brace before each rep; breathe steadily. Prioritize form over speed or extra reps.

steps: HIIT Wed: 40 s work / 20 s rest × 8 rounds, 3 exercises
Alternate A/B exercises every 4 weeks for variety
Track RPE — mesomorphs recover fast but watch joints
tips: Eat to match training day (more carbs on lower days)
Deload when performance drops 2 sessions in a row
Warm up shoulders before heavy pressing

mesoSport.name: Athletic power (mesomorph)
mesoSport.summary: Plyometrics and sport drills by movement pattern and muscle group.
mesoSport.description: Develops explosive strength in legs and core, rotational power in hips and shoulders, and posterior chain for sprinting. ACSM: include power training 2–3×/week for athletic populations.
warmup: High knees, butt kicks, A-skips — 20 m each

items: Box jump — 4×5, land soft, step down
quads.detail: Power work needs full rest between sets. Stop if landing gets loud or knees cave. Track knees over toes. Depth depends on mobility — keep a neutral spine.

hamstrings.detail: Power work needs full rest between sets. Stop if landing gets loud or knees cave. Hinge at the hips on RDL/deadlift patterns. Feel stretch, then drive hips forward.

glutes.detail: Power work needs full rest between sets. Stop if landing gets loud or knees cave. Pause and squeeze at lockout. Avoid hyperextending the lower back.

calves.detail: Power work needs full rest between sets. Stop if landing gets loud or knees cave. Full stretch at the bottom, peak contraction at the top. Use a slow tempo.

core.detail: Power work needs full rest between sets. Stop if landing gets loud or knees cave. Brace before each rep; breathe steadily. Prioritize form over speed or extra reps.

shoulders.detail: Power work needs full rest between sets. Stop if landing gets loud or knees cave. Press for strength, raises for volume, rear-delt work for shoulder health. Keep wrists neutral.

back.detail: Power work needs full rest between sets. Stop if landing gets loud or knees cave. Pair vertical and horizontal pulls. Squeeze shoulder blades; avoid swinging or using momentum.

steps: Limit plyo foot contacts to 80–100/session beginner
Full recovery (2–3 min) between power sets
Sport day: 60 min skill + moderate intensity scrimmage
tips: Land quietly on jumps — knee alignment over toes
No plyometrics on consecutive days
Replace sport day with rest if joints feel beat up

endoCardio.name: Fat-burn cardio (endomorph)
endoCardio.summary: High-frequency lower-body cardio with core stability work.
endoCardio.description: Endomorphs benefit from daily lower-body movement (biggest muscle groups burn most calories). WHO 150–300 min/week; start at 150 and progress. Pair with strength on alternate days.
warmup: Hip circles, knee circles — 10 each direction
weeklySplit: Daily — 30 min brisk walk (NEAT goal 8–10k steps)
Wed — Swimming or elliptical 45 min (full leg)
Sat — Light hike or long walk 60 min

items: Recumbent or upright bike — 35–45 min, moderate resistance
Water walking (pool) — 20 min if joint pain
quads.detail: Consistency beats intensity. Add minutes before adding speed or incline. Track knees over toes. Depth depends on mobility — keep a neutral spine.

hamstrings.note: Elliptical and rowing engage posterior chain
items: Elliptical backward stride — 10 min within session
hamstrings.detail: Consistency beats intensity. Add minutes before adding speed or incline. Hinge at the hips on RDL/deadlift patterns. Feel stretch, then drive hips forward.

glutes.note: Hills and stairs — high calorie burn
items: Stair climber — 20 min (hold rails lightly, posture tall)
glutes.detail: Consistency beats intensity. Add minutes before adding speed or incline. Pause and squeeze at lockout. Avoid hyperextending the lower back.

calves.detail: Consistency beats intensity. Add minutes before adding speed or incline. Full stretch at the bottom, peak contraction at the top. Use a slow tempo.

core.detail: Consistency beats intensity. Add minutes before adding speed or incline. Brace before each rep; breathe steadily. Prioritize form over speed or extra reps.

steps: Week 1–2: 150 min total; add 15 min/week until 300 min
Use talk test — slightly breathless but can speak
Log steps — aim +500/day each week until 10k
tips: Consistency beats intensity for fat loss
Protein at every meal preserves muscle
See doctor before jogging if BMI > 30 or knee pain

endoStrength.name: Full-body strength (endomorph)
endoStrength.summary: Circuit training hitting every muscle group each session.
endoStrength.description: Full-body sessions maximize calorie burn per workout and hit each muscle group 2–3×/week. Compound movements first, 45–60 s rest in circuits. ACSM: resistance 2+ days/week for all adults; critical for endomorphs to preserve lean mass during fat loss.

quads.note: Every session — squat or lunge pattern
quads.detail: Full-body circuits: move with control, rest 45–60 s between rounds. Track knees over toes. Depth depends on mobility — keep a neutral spine.

glutes.note: Drive hips through on every rep
glutes.detail: Full-body circuits: move with control, rest 45–60 s between rounds. Pause and squeeze at lockout. Avoid hyperextending the lower back.

chest.detail: Full-body circuits: move with control, rest 45–60 s between rounds. Order: compound press first, then incline, finish with fly/isolation. Rest as noted between heavy sets.

back.detail: Full-body circuits: move with control, rest 45–60 s between rounds. Pair vertical and horizontal pulls. Squeeze shoulder blades; avoid swinging or using momentum.

shoulders.detail: Full-body circuits: move with control, rest 45–60 s between rounds. Press for strength, raises for volume, rear-delt work for shoulder health. Keep wrists neutral.

core.detail: Full-body circuits: move with control, rest 45–60 s between rounds. Brace before each rep; breathe steadily. Prioritize form over speed or extra reps.

biceps.detail: Full-body circuits: move with control, rest 45–60 s between rounds. Full range of motion, elbows fixed. Two to three exercises per session is enough.

triceps.detail: Full-body circuits: move with control, rest 45–60 s between rounds. Mix pressing and extension patterns. Lock out under control without flaring elbows.

hamstrings.detail: Full-body circuits: move with control, rest 45–60 s between rounds. Hinge at the hips on RDL/deadlift patterns. Feel stretch, then drive hips forward.

items: Hold top stretch 2 s each rep
calves.detail: Full-body circuits: move with control, rest 45–60 s between rounds. Full stretch at the bottom, peak contraction at the top. Use a slow tempo.

steps: Run as circuit: 3–4 exercises back-to-back, rest 90 s, repeat 3 rounds
Increase weight when top of rep range feels easy
Keep sessions under 50 min for adherence
tips: Form over weight — injury stops all progress
Eat protein 1.6–2 g/kg on training days
Walk 10 min post-meal on rest days (glucose control)

mobilityAll.name: Mobility & recovery (all types)
mobilityAll.summary: Stretching and activation by muscle group — daily routine.
mobilityAll.description: ACSM recommends flexibility ≥2–3 days/week; daily mobility improves recovery between muscle-group sessions. Hold static stretches after training or warm shower. Never bounce.
weeklySplit: Extra 5 min on leg day: quads + hamstrings + glutes
Extra 5 min on upper day: chest + shoulders + back

quads.note: Hip extension required for full quad stretch
quads.detail: Hold stretches 30–45 s, breathe into tension. Never force painful range. Track knees over toes. Depth depends on mobility — keep a neutral spine.

hamstrings.note: Keep slight knee bend if lower back rounds
hamstrings.detail: Hold stretches 30–45 s, breathe into tension. Never force painful range. Hinge at the hips on RDL/deadlift patterns. Feel stretch, then drive hips forward.

glutes.detail: Hold stretches 30–45 s, breathe into tension. Never force painful range. Pause and squeeze at lockout. Avoid hyperextending the lower back.

core.detail: Hold stretches 30–45 s, breathe into tension. Never force painful range. Brace before each rep; breathe steadily. Prioritize form over speed or extra reps.

back.detail: Hold stretches 30–45 s, breathe into tension. Never force painful range. Pair vertical and horizontal pulls. Squeeze shoulder blades; avoid swinging or using momentum.

shoulders.detail: Hold stretches 30–45 s, breathe into tension. Never force painful range. Press for strength, raises for volume, rear-delt work for shoulder health. Keep wrists neutral.

chest.detail: Hold stretches 30–45 s, breathe into tension. Never force painful range. Order: compound press first, then incline, finish with fly/isolation. Rest as noted between heavy sets.

steps: Breathe into stretch — exhale to go deeper
Do after workouts or before bed
Add yoga class 1×/week for full-body flow
tips: Pain ≠ gain — mild tension only, no sharp pain
Hypermobile people: focus stability over stretch
Consistency daily beats one long session weekly
